title: Determine the exact value of R(C4, C4, K4)
code_used: Tiny bounded experiment only: exhaustive check of the simplest cyclic 4x5 block template with one shift-matching per block pair; no full search, SAT, ILP, or brute-force coloring census.
micro_paper_assessment: Still a strong micro-paper target if solved, but this solve only isolates a rigorous 20->10 neighborhood reduction and rules out the simplest cyclic four-K5 lower-bound template. The current package is still too thin for the micro-paper lane.
generalization_signal: moderate: the density-to-neighborhood reduction should transfer to nearby (C4, C4, K_t) triples, but the sharp local obstruction remains unresolved.
candidate_theorem_slice: If every 10-vertex 3-coloring with no red C4 and no blue C4 contains a green triangle, then every 20-vertex 3-coloring contains a red C4, a blue C4, or a green K4; hence R(C4, C4, K4) = 20.
next_action: Prioritize the exact local obstruction R(C4, C4, K3) or a stronger green-degree lemma. If the lower-bound side is revisited, move only to slightly richer affine/incidence-style block interfaces, not broad unconstrained search.
